layman_headline: The market is bleeding — no good swing trade setups right now, staying in cash.
layman_explanation: Almost every cryptocurrency is down today, with Bitcoin falling over 3% and many altcoins down even more. The overall market mood is fearful (28/100), and Bitcoin dominance is high at 55.9%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins. None of the swing trade candidates have a convincing combination of technical strength and catalyst to justify risking capital right now.
layman_action: Keep your cash on the sidelines. Wait for the market to stabilize and for clearer buy signals to emerge before entering any new positions.
expert_reasoning: In a Fear environment (28) with BTC dropping -3.4% in 24h and dominance at 55.9%, altcoin entries carry elevated risk. GRASS shows the sharpest 7-day decline (-31%) suggesting potential mean reversion, but the bearish engulfing candle and -0.9 chart bias indicate selling pressure isn't exhausted. LIT's RSI of 72 after a +55.8% 5-day run is a classic overbought rejection setup — not a buy. MON and PENGU lack catalysts and have bearish structures. The CoinDesk signal about Bitcoin panic-selling margins disappearing could indicate a BTC bottom forming, but we need confirmation before committing capital to alts. Prudent to wait for either RSI oversold readings or volume breakouts with confirmed support holds.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $60K support triggering cascading alt liquidations
Geopolitical escalation (Hormuz Strait) creating broader risk-off moves across all assets
High BTC dominance trend may continue squeezing altcoin valuations further
watch_list: BTC price action around $60,000 support — a hold here could trigger alt recovery
GRASS stabilization near $0.30 support after -31% weekly drop — potential mean-reversion if selling exhausts
Fear & Greed index trend — currently improving from 24.6 weekly avg to 28, a move above 35 would improve entry conditions
market_context: Broad risk-off environment with BTC threatening $60K, elevated geopolitical tensions, and Strategy pausing its Bitcoin buying program. Fear is dominant but improving slightly from extreme levels, suggesting a potential bottom could be forming over the next few days.
context_banner: Market in fear-driven correction with no high-conviction swing setups — capital preservation is the priority.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from 24.6 weekly average — not yet signaling extreme fear capitulation that typically marks reliable bottoms.
Bitcoin panic-selling margins disappearing suggests sellers are running out of profitable exits, which could indicate a bottom forming but needs price confirmation.
Trump invoking geopolitical pressure on Hormuz Strait is creating risk-off sentiment across crypto, pushing BTC toward $62K and dragging alts lower.
No candidate shows oversold RSI — best reading is MON at 41 which is approaching but not at oversold levels, while LIT at 72 is overbought and risky.
All candidates show bearish or neutral chart bias scores ranging from -1.4 (KITE) to +0.4 (LIT), with no volume-confirmed breakout setups present.
